title: AWS Transfer Manager, Concurrency, and Migration · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for aws transfer manager, concurrency, and migration and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for transfer-manager adoption and resource budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Adopt feature/s3/transfermanager 0.3.2 as a pinned pre-v1 dependency with explicit compatibility review and rollback.
Inventory deprecated feature/s3/manager use, map behavior and options, characterize outputs, and migrate behind contract tests.
Choose UploadObject, DownloadObject, and GetObject by streaming, seekability, checksum, destination, and ownership requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Transfermanager Version Status · changed case · Quiz Transfermanager Version…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 4907 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to adopt transfermanager 0.3.2 while rejecting deprecated use and bounding aggregate concurrency; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Transfermanager Version Status without dropping earlier contracts?
